questions.question_text: Under Rhode Island law, all salespersons must conduct business through whom?
distractors: Any managing associate
The state real estate commission
A licensed attorney
questions.explanation: Rhode Island requires all salespersons to work under and conduct all business through a licensed broker. Salespersons cannot operate independently or under an unlicensed entity.

questions.question_text: A salesperson negotiates a transaction outside the scope of their broker's authorization. Who may be held liable?
questions.correct_answer: The broker for the salesperson's actions
distractors: Only the salesperson personally
The buyer for not verifying authority
The title company that processed it
questions.explanation: In Rhode Island, the broker is liable for actions of supervised salespersons within the scope of employment. Even unauthorized actions can create broker liability if they appear to fall within normal duties.

questions.question_text: Who is responsible for establishing and enforcing office policies in a Rhode Island brokerage?
distractors: Each individual salesperson
The state licensing board
The office manager only
questions.explanation: The broker is responsible for establishing and enforcing office policies that govern how the brokerage operates. This includes policies on advertising, trust accounts, agency disclosure, and all aspects of practice.

questions.question_text: A new salesperson wants to advertise listings on social media using only their personal name. Is this permitted in Rhode Island?
questions.correct_answer: No, all ads must go through the broker
distractors: Yes, if the salesperson pays for the ads
Yes, with verbal broker approval only
Only if the listing is their own listing
questions.explanation: All real estate business must be conducted through the licensed broker. Advertising without proper broker identification violates Rhode Island supervision requirements and advertising rules.

questions.question_text: The principal broker of a Rhode Island firm discovers a salesperson has been failing to present the MRED form. What is the PB's primary obligation?
questions.correct_answer: Enforce compliance and correct the issue
distractors: Immediately terminate the salesperson from the firm
File a lawsuit against the salesperson personally
Report only if an outside complaint is filed
questions.explanation: The principal broker oversees firm operations and compliance. Upon discovering a violation, the PB must take corrective action to enforce policies and ensure all licensees comply with Rhode Island disclosure requirements.

questions.question_text: How long must a Rhode Island broker maintain escrow records?
distractors: At least 1 year
At least 5 years
At least 7 years
questions.explanation: Rhode Island requires brokers to maintain escrow records for at least 3 years. These records must document who the money belongs to, deposit and withdrawal dates, payees, and other pertinent information.

questions.question_text: A Rhode Island broker is audited and asked to produce transaction files from 18 months ago. Which information must the escrow records contain?
questions.correct_answer: Fund ownership, deposit dates, and payees
distractors: Only the final closing statement from the deal
Just the property address and the sale price
Only canceled checks from the relevant period
questions.explanation: Rhode Island escrow records must show who the money belongs to, deposit and withdrawal dates, payees, and pertinent information about each transaction. All of this must be maintained for at least 3 years.

questions.question_text: Which broker duty includes ensuring that all contracts and documents are properly completed and delivered?
distractors: Marketing and advertising oversight duties
Commission distribution management for agents
Continuing education compliance verification
questions.explanation: Rhode Island brokers have a duty to ensure proper document handling, execution, delivery, and retention. This includes ensuring all transaction documents are correctly completed, signed, and distributed to appropriate parties.

questions.question_text: A broker destroys transaction files after two years to save storage space. What issue does this create?
distractors: No issue if digital copies exist elsewhere
It is acceptable for closed transactions
Only escrow files need longer retention
questions.explanation: Rhode Island requires brokers to maintain transaction and escrow records for at least 3 years. Destroying files after only 2 years violates this requirement regardless of the transaction status.

questions.question_text: A salesperson receives an earnest money deposit but the broker is out of town. What is the correct procedure?
questions.correct_answer: Deposit through the broker's established process
distractors: Hold the check until the broker returns
Deposit into the salesperson's personal account
Return the deposit to the buyer temporarily
questions.explanation: All business must be conducted through the licensed broker. The broker should have established procedures for handling deposits in their absence. A salesperson must never deposit client funds into a personal account.
